name: Raspberry Pi 5
description: BCM2712, the reference SBC for hobbyist Linux

release-date.comment: Announced 2023-09-28; first units shipped end of October 2023.

status.comment: Raspberry Pi Ltd guarantees production through January 2036.

open-source-hardware.comment: Raspberry Pi publishes mechanical drawings and a partial schematic, but PCB design files are not released under an OSHW-compatible license.

country-of-origin.comment: Designed in Cambridge, UK; manufactured at the Sony UK Technology Centre in Pencoed, South Wales.

soc-vendor.comment: BCM2712 was designed in-house by Raspberry Pi but is fabbed and branded as Broadcom — vendor tag 'broadcom' matches the silicon brand.

process-node.comment: TSMC 16nm process node per multiple secondary sources; Raspberry Pi has not published the exact node themselves.

npu.comment: No onboard NPU; AI HAT+ accessory adds a Hailo-8/8L NPU but this is an add-on.

hw-video-decode.comment: BCM2712 does HEVC/H.265 4K60 in hardware. Unlike BCM2711 on the Pi 4, hardware H.264 decode was dropped — H.264, VP9, and AV1 are software-decoded on the CPU.

hw-video-encode.comment: No hardware video encoder on Pi 5; encoding (H.264/H.265) is CPU-only.

ram-min-gb.comment: 1 GB SKU added later (alongside the 2 GB cost-down variant); SKUs sold are 1, 2, 4, 8, and 16 GB.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: No onboard M.2 slot; PCIe 2.0 x1 is exposed via an FFC connector and an official M.2 HAT+ accessory adds an M.2 2230/2242 slot.

ethernet.comment: Single Gigabit Ethernet port with PoE+ support via separate HAT.

wifi.comment: Dual-band 802.11ac (Wi-Fi 5) via Infineon CYW43455 combo chip.

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: 2× USB 3.0 with simultaneous 5 Gbps operation (Pi 4 had to share one controller across both).

usb-c-ports-data.comment: USB-C port is power-only (5V/5A PD); no USB data on the Type-C connector.

poe-support.comment: PoE+ available only through the official PoE+ HAT.

displayport.comment: No DisplayPort or DP-Alt mode (USB-C is power-only).

mipi-dsi.comment: 2× combined CSI/DSI 22-pin connectors — either can act as a DSI display port.

mipi-csi.comment: 2× combined CSI/DSI 22-pin connectors — either can act as a CSI camera port.

audio-jack.comment: Pi 5 dropped the 3.5 mm AV jack present on the Pi 4; documentation references the AV jack inconsistently but the official product brief and product page list no analog jack on the Pi 5 board itself.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Mechanically and electrically HAT-compatible; some older HATs needing the 3.5 mm jack or specific board height may have fit issues.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: Single-lane PCIe 2.0 over an FFC/FPC connector; an official M.2 HAT+ adapts this to an M.2 NVMe slot.

power-input.comment: Official 27 W PSU recommended for full 5A/5V; lower-power supplies clamp peripheral current.

idle-power-w.comment: ~3 W idle (4 GB variant) measured by Jeff Geerling with the official 5A PSU; 2 GB variant ~2.4 W. Varies ±1 W with PSU choice.

load-power-w.comment: BCM2712 TDP ~12 W; full-load wall-plug draw with peripherals typically ~9–12 W per Geerling measurements.

cooling-required.comment: Raspberry Pi sells an Active Cooler (heatsink + fan) and recommends active cooling for sustained max load; passive heatsinking will throttle. The board has a JST-SH PWM fan header for the official cooler.

rtc-battery-header.comment: First flagship Pi with an onboard RTC and a 2-pin battery connector.

length-mm.comment: PCB length 85 mm (slightly varying figures in the wild — 85 mm is the official mechanical drawing dimension).

width-mm.comment: PCB width 56 mm — same Raspberry Pi standard footprint as the Pi 4.

weight-g.comment: Bare board weight ~46 g (retailers report 46–50 g depending on revision).

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Mainline boots (kernel 6.12+ with SUSE-led upstreaming) but RP1 South Bridge support — including Ethernet, USB, GPIO — is still being upstreamed; vendor downstream kernel remains required for full hardware. As of April 2026 mainline support is improving but not yet at parity.

official-os-image.comment: Raspberry Pi OS (Debian-based) published and actively maintained.

distros-with-images.comment: Raspberry Pi OS (official), Ubuntu (Canonical-published 24.04+ images), Debian (community/raspi.debian.net), Fedora (aarch64), Alpine, DietPi, Manjaro ARM all publish Pi 5 images. Armbian historically does not target the Pi family.

bootloader.comment: Pi 5 uses Raspberry Pi's proprietary closed-source bootloader stored in onboard SPI flash; it loads Linux directly without U-Boot in the default flow.

long-term-support.comment: Production guaranteed through January 2036 — over a decade of expected hardware availability and matching kernel/OS support.

android-support.comment: No official Android image; community LineageOS/KonstaKANG ports exist but are unofficial.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Original launch MSRP for the 4 GB SKU was $60 (October 2023); a 2 GB SKU was later added at $50. Recent (early-2026) memory-driven price hikes have pushed street prices higher — note that street price as of April 2026 commonly exceeds MSRP by 50%+.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Original 16 GB MSRP $120 at launch (Jan 2025). Tom's Hardware reports street/announced prices have climbed to ~$205+ as of early 2026 due to LPDDR4 supply pressure. MSRP figure here reflects original sticker price.

available-regions.comment: Sold globally through the Approved Reseller network.

typical-lead-time.comment: Snapshot 2026-04-26: in stock at major resellers (The Pi Hut, Adafruit, CanaKit), though pricing is volatile.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive official documentation, mechanical drawings, datasheet/product brief, BCM2712 docs, and beginner-friendly Getting Started materials.

community-size.comment: Per RESEARCH.md threshold: 'huge' is reserved for Raspberry Pi only.

active-forum-discord.comment: Official Raspberry Pi Forums are extremely active and vendor-staffed.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Largest SBC accessory ecosystem in the world: official Active Cooler, Case, AI HAT+, M.2 HAT+, PoE+ HAT, Camera Modules, Touch Display 2, and a vast third-party HAT/case/screen market.
